title: No Trample+
description: Protect your farmland from being trampled. No matter how high you fall, your crops stay safe.
body:
![No Trample+](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/242f6e36bace5d7606698841a5b48b2c532e8c50.jpeg)

**Protect your farmland from being trampled.
No matter how high you fall, your crops stay safe.**

Protect your farmland from being trampled.
No matter how high you fall, your crops stay safe.



No Trample is a simple and lightweight data pack that prevents farmland from being destroyed when stepped or landed on.

Unlike some data packs, **this one does NOT use Feather Falling or modify fall damage**. Players will still take full damage when falling from height — only the farmland is protected.

Whether you’re farming, exploring, or building, you no longer have to worry about accidentally ruining your crops. Even from great heights, farmland will remain intact and plants will not break.

The data pack works for both players and entities, with separate options to enable or disable each behavior.

## Features
- No Fall Damage Changes - Fall damage remains completely vanilla
- Farmland Protection - Prevents trampling from any height
- Player Toggle - Enable or disable trampling prevention for players
- Entity Toggle - Enable or disable for other entities (animals, mobs, etc.)
- Load Message Toggle - Turn the startup message on or off
- Reset Option - Restore all settings to default

## Requirements:

Minecraft Java Edition 1.21.5-26.1.2

Installation:

Singleplayer
- Open Minecraft
- Create a new world and click on "More", "Data Packs"
- Drag the data pack into the Minecraft window
- Click "Yes" on the confirmation screen
- Move the data pack to the right-hand side of the screen by clicking the triangle on its icon
- Click "Done" and continue creating your world, then click on "Create New World"

Multiplayer
- Download the data pack
- Open your server folder, then open the folder of the world
- Put the data pack into a folder named datapacks
- Restart your server, and you can use your new data pack.

Permissions:
None

Commands:
/trigger trample.options
